method: ran the proof as a subprocess (unsealed (isolation='none')); verdict = exit code (0 → green, else red)

evidence.stdout_tail:
PASS  test_the_stamp_is_the_sixth_place_after_the_decimal
  PASS  test_the_gate_issues_a_thin_breadcrumb_pointing_to_the_ticket
  PASS  test_it_sends_home_to_the_wired_receiver_in_order
  PASS  test_entries_for_one_ticket_share_the_pointer_and_order_by_stamp
  PASS  test_unwired_emit_is_held_not_lost
  PASS  test_a_value_snapshot_rides_only_when_watching_values
  PASS  test_every_device_inherits_emit_structurally
  PASS  test_the_breadcrumb_is_data_the_interpreter_can_crawl
green — the gate issues a thin breadcrumb (pointer to the ticket, µs-stamped), sends it home to CC's mailbox, holds it loud when homeless, and the whole mailbox is DATA the interpreter crawls — every device inherits emit structurally (Law 2)

seal.detail: none: no seal requested — the route is open by construction, and the record says so

falsifier: the same proof exits non-zero on re-run, or the code it proves changes underneath it
horizon: valid until the proof file or the code it proves changes (Law 3: a VALIDATION expires)
